title: Top 8 Finalist · Code-A-Thon 2.0
description: Out of 100+ submissions, Singularity placed Top 8. Built over 1.5 months — not a weekend sprint — it pulled real-time telemetry from NASA, SpaceX, and ISRO into a single mission control. Orbital mechanics, 3D globe rendering, AI integration, live ISS tracking. The kind of project you build when you actually care about what you're making.

title: Shipped to 500+ Users · SIMS
description: Most portfolio projects live on localhost. SIMS lives at a college domain and gets used by over 500 students every day. Built during my internship under the HOD — I owned the entire frontend. Seeing something you built actually get used is a different kind of validation.

title: GitHub Workshop · Volunteer
description: Volunteered at a Git & GitHub workshop for third-year students. Helped them get comfortable with version control, branching, and collaborative development. Teaching something is the fastest way to find out how well you actually know it.

title: 8.365 GPA · B.Tech
description: Balancing active development, hackathons, freelance work, and an internship — while holding an 8.365. The academics and the building happen in parallel, not instead of each other.
